# BudgetLens

> Privacy-first personal finance app for uploading and analyzing bank/credit card statements. No bank login required — upload CSV or PDF statements directly.

## What it does

BudgetLens lets users upload their bank and credit card statements, automatically categorizes every transaction, and provides AI-powered financial insights and Q&A. All data stays in the user's account — no third-party bank connections.

## Pages and features

### / (Home / Landing)
Public marketing page. Explains the app, sign up / log in links.

### /login
Email + password authentication. JWT-based sessions stored in browser.

### /dashboard
Main financial overview for a selected month. Shows:
- Total income, total spending, net savings, savings rate
- Spending breakdown by category (donut chart)
- Month-over-month trend bar chart
- Top merchants by spend
- Month navigator (previous / next)
- Categorization gate — prompts user to categorize before unlocking full analysis

### /upload
Upload bank or credit card statements (CSV or PDF). Supports:
- Multiple accounts (checking, savings, credit card, etc.)
- Auto-detection of statement format
- Duplicate detection across uploads
- Staging preview before committing transactions

### /upload/[id]
Upload detail page for a specific upload session.

### /staging/[uploadId]
Pre-commit review of parsed transactions from an upload. User can:
- Review all detected transactions
- Filter by status (categorized / needs review / uncategorized)
- Manually assign categories before saving
- Flag duplicates or transfers
- Auto-categorize using AI

### /transactions
Full transaction list across all accounts and months. Supports:
- Search by merchant name
- Filter by category, account, date range
- Sort by date, amount, merchant
- Click any transaction for detail

### /transactions/[id]
Single transaction detail view. Shows merchant, amount, date, category, account, and allows manual override of category.

### /categorize
Drag-and-drop transaction categorization workspace. Features:
- Uncategorized transactions on the left
- Category buckets on the right
- Drag transactions into categories
- Bulk categorize by merchant
- AI-assisted category suggestions
- Save rules from categorization actions

### /categories
Manage spending categories. Create, rename, reorder, or delete categories. Categories are user-defined — no forced preset structure.

### /rules
Auto-categorization rules engine. Rules map merchant names (exact or pattern match) to categories. Rules run automatically on new uploads. Supports:
- Keyword/merchant match rules
- Category assignment
- Enable/disable individual rules

### /history
Upload history log. Shows all past statement uploads with status, date, account, and transaction count. Click to review or re-process an upload.

### /insights (Q&A)
AI-powered financial Q&A powered by OpenAI GPT-4o. Features:
- Ask natural language questions about spending (e.g. "Where did most of my money go?")
- Full access to user's transaction data, category totals, merchant stats, subscriptions
- Web search integration (Tavily) for real-time price comparisons and local deals
- Month-specific context — switch months to ask about different periods
- Conversation history maintained across messages
- Starter prompts for common questions
- Inline number chips with "view transactions" deep-links
- Markdown-rendered responses (headers, bullets, bold)

### /triage
Flagged transaction review — transactions that need manual attention (ambiguous merchant, possible duplicate, unusual amount).

## Tech stack

- Next.js 14 (App Router), React 18, TypeScript
- Prisma ORM + PostgreSQL (Neon)
- Custom JWT auth (bcryptjs) — no Clerk, no OAuth
- Tailwind CSS + CSS custom properties (dark/light theme)
- TanStack Query for data fetching
- OpenAI GPT-4o for AI chat
- Tavily Search API for real-time web search
- Recharts for data visualization
- Vercel deployment

## Privacy model

- No bank login or OAuth connection required
- Users upload statement files directly — files are parsed and discarded
- No plaintext email stored — email hashed (SHA-256)
- All data is per-user and never shared
- Local-first design: the user controls their own data

## Deployment

Live at: budgetapp-lyqzujzll-gascancharlies-projects.vercel.app
Source: github.com/GasCanCharlie/budget-app
